description: Standardized consolidated findings fixture for fixer benchmark. Based on Phase 3 Sonnet/high run consolidated output. All 9 seeded issues represented. Consolidator score: 47/100.

findings.location: Overview paragraph 1 vs Stage 2 — Transformation paragraph 1
findings.description: Direct logical contradiction: Overview states all pipeline stages run in parallel; Stage 2 requires Stage 2 to complete before Stage 3 begins.
findings.evidence: Overview: 'All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput.' Stage 2: 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins.'
findings.suggestion: Remove the blanket parallelism claim. Replace with accurate description: 'Ingestion and transformation stages operate sequentially; delivery begins only after transformation is complete.'

findings.description: Dangling cross-reference to non-existent Section 6. The document has no Section 6 and sections are not numbered.
findings.evidence: 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' No Section 6 exists.
findings.suggestion: Add a Security Considerations section addressing authentication, encryption in transit, encryption at rest, and access control — OR remove the reference and note security is out of scope.

findings.description: Batch/streaming performance claim is factually inverted. Streaming outperforms batch for small files; batch is preferred for large payloads.
findings.evidence: 'Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB. For larger payloads, streaming is preferred.' Both sentences are inverted.
findings.suggestion: Correct to: 'Internal benchmarks show that streaming is 3× faster than batch for files under 10MB. For larger payloads, batch processing is preferred.'

findings.description: 99.9% latency reduction claim is extraordinary and unsupported. No baseline, methodology, test conditions, or citation.
findings.evidence: 'The in-memory delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes.'
findings.suggestion: Remove the percentage. Use qualified language: 'significantly reduces end-to-end write latency.' If real data exists, cite it with conditions.

findings.description: 'Standard retry policy' referenced but never defined in the document.
findings.evidence: 'Transformation applies the standard retry policy on transient failures.' No retry parameters defined.
findings.suggestion: Define inline: 'applies an exponential backoff retry policy (up to 3 attempts, doubling delay on each transient failure)'

findings.description: 'Large files' threshold undefined — operationally meaningless without a size boundary.
findings.evidence: 'Large files are handled separately from the primary transformation path via an overflow queue.' No size threshold given.
findings.suggestion: Define threshold: 'Files exceeding 100MB are handled separately...'

findings.description: 'Pipeline' and 'workflow' used interchangeably and inconsistently throughout without definition.
findings.evidence: Operational Notes: 'The pipeline and workflow are monitored.' Conclusions: 'real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline.'
findings.suggestion: Choose one term and use consistently, or define both explicitly.

findings.description: ETL acronym not expanded on first use.
findings.evidence: 'an internal ETL system' — ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) not expanded.
findings.suggestion: Expand on first use: 'an internal ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) system'

findings.description: Authentication mechanism unspecified in a final architecture brief.
findings.evidence: 'Authentication is used to verify each request before processing begins.' No type specified.
findings.suggestion: Specify mechanism: e.g., 'API key authentication is used...' or mark TBD.
